Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: On page 3, under the 'RESULTS' section, the text states: 'As of the data cutoff on October 14, 2018, median follow-up time was 14.4 months.' This is the median follow-up duration.

Treatment Arm(s).value: enzalutamide plus ADT; placebo plus ADT
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: "1,150 men with mHSPC were randomly assigned 1:1 to enzalutamide (160 mg/day) or placebo, plus androgen deprivation therapy (ADT)". This is further confirmed in the 'Study Design and Conduct' section on page 2 and in Table 1 and Figure 1, which list the arms as "Enzalutamide Plus ADT" and "Placebo Plus ADT".

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states: '...wherein 1,150 men with mHSPC were randomly assigned 1:1 to enzalutamide (160 mg/day) or placebo, plus androgen deprivation therapy (ADT)...'. This is further confirmed on page 2 under 'Patients and Treatments': 'Patients were centrally randomized 1:1 to enzalutamide (160 mg/day) plus ADT or placebo plus ADT...'.

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'ARCHES (...) is a multinational, double-blind, phase III trial, wherein 1,150 men with mHSPC were randomly assigned 1:1 to enzalutamide (160 mg/day) or placebo, plus androgen deprivation therapy (ADT)'. This is further confirmed on page 3 under 'RESULTS - Patients and Treatment': 'From March 21, 2016, to January 12, 2018, a total of 1,150 patients were randomly assigned 1:1'. Figure 1 (CONSORT diagram) on page 4 also shows 'Randomly assigned 1:1* (n = 1,150)' and then breaks it down into 'Allocated to enzalutamide plus ADT, ie, ITT population (n = 574)' and 'Allocated to placebo plus ADT, ie, ITT population (n = 576)', which sum up to 1,150.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: On page 1, in the 'PURPOSE' section, enzalutamide is described as 'a potent androgen-receptor inhibitor'. The 'METHODS' section states that patients were assigned to 'enzalutamide (160 mg/day) or placebo, plus androgen deprivation therapy (ADT)'. Enzalutamide is the experimental agent in Treatment Arm 1, and its class is an androgen-receptor inhibitor.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Control Arm - N.evidence: On page 4, Figure 1 (CONSORT diagram) under 'Allocation', it states 'Allocated to placebo plus ADT, ie, ITT population (n = 576)'. This is further confirmed in Table 1 'Baseline Demographics' on page 5, which lists 'Placebo Plus ADT (n = 576)'.

Control Arm.evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states: '...randomly assigned 1:1 to enzalutamide (160 mg/day) or placebo, plus androgen deprivation therapy (ADT)...'. The placebo group serves as the control arm.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: '...wherein 1,150 men with mHSPC were randomly assigned 1:1 to enzalutamide (160 mg/day) or placebo, plus androgen deprivation therapy (ADT)...'. This indicates two treatment arms: enzalutamide plus ADT and placebo plus ADT. The CONSORT diagram on page 4 further confirms this with 'Allocated to enzalutamide plus ADT' and 'Allocated to placebo plus ADT'.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states that '1,150 men with mHSPC were randomly assigned 1:1 to enzalutamide (160 mg/day) or placebo, plus androgen deprivation therapy (ADT)'. The CONSORT diagram (FIG 1) on page 4 further specifies 'Allocated to enzalutamide plus ADT, ie, ITT population (n = 574)' and 'Allocated to placebo plus ADT, ie, ITT population (n = 576)'. The experimental arm is Enzalutamide Plus ADT, with 574 participants.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: Found in the 'Safety' section on page 4: 'Median treatment duration was 12.8 months (range, 0.2 to 26.6 months) in the enzalutamide plus ADT group'.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: Found in the 'Safety' section on page 4: 'and 11.6 months (range, 0.2 to 24.6 months) in the placebo plus ADT group'.

Trial.evidence: The title of the document is "ARCHES: A Randomized, Phase III Study of Androgen Deprivation Therapy With Enzalutamide or Placebo in Men With Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer". The 'METHODS' section on page 1 also states, "ARCHES (ClinicalTrials.gov identifier: NCT02677896) is a multinational, double-blind, phase III trial..."

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: The value '70.0' is found in 'TABLE 1. Baseline Demographics' on page 5, under the 'Enzalutamide Plus ADT (n = 574)' column, row 'Age (years) | Median'.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: The value '70.0' is found in 'TABLE 1. Baseline Demographics' on page 5, under the 'Placebo Plus ADT (n = 576)' column, row 'Age (years) | Median'.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 states: 'Patients in both treatment groups reported a high baseline level of quality of life, which was maintained over time.' Additionally, Table 2 on page 7, under 'Other secondary end points', reports 'Median time to deterioration of QoL (months)' with values for both treatment groups (11.3 and 11.1 months, respectively) and a P-value of .6548. The 'DISCUSSION' section on page 11 also confirms that 'no significant difference between treatment groups in risk of deterioration of urinary symptoms or QoL was observed, suggesting there was no negative impact on PROs due to the addition of enzalutamide to ADT' and that the trial maintained 'the high level of QoL reported at baseline'.

NCT.evidence: Found under "Clinical trial information" and in the "METHODS" section on page 1, explicitly stating "ClinicalTrials.gov identifier: NCT02677896".

PubMed ID.evidence: No PubMed ID was explicitly reported in the document.

Year.evidence: Found in the publication date information on page 1: "Accepted on June 6, 2019 and published at jco.org on July 22, 2019" and "J Clin Oncol 37:2974-2986. 2019".

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document contains extensive sections including methods, results, and discussion, spanning multiple pages (2974-2986), indicating it is a full publication rather than just an abstract.

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The document is titled "ARCHES: A Randomized, Phase III Study..." and has a "original reports" label on page 1, indicating it is the initial report of the trial results.

Quality of Life Scale.value: Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (FACT-P) and Quality of Life Prostate-Specific questionnaire (QLQ-PR25)
Quality of Life Scale.evidence: On page 3, in the 'Assessments' section, it states: 'PRO assessments, such as Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate, Quality of Life Prostate-Specific questionnaire and Brief Pain Inventory-Short Form (BPI-SF), were completed at baseline, week 13, and every 12 weeks thereafter.' Footnotes h and i for TABLE 1 on page 6 further clarify that 'QLQ-PR25' refers to the 'Quality of Life Prostate-Specific questionnaire' and 'FACT-P' refers to the 'Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate'.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: On page 6, in TABLE 1. Baseline Demographics (continued), under 'No. of cycles of prior docetaxel chemotherapy' for 'Enzalutamide Plus ADT (n = 574)', the values are 14 (2.4%) for '1-5' cycles and 89 (15.5%) for '6' cycles. Summing these gives 14 + 89 = 103 participants, and 2.4% + 15.5% = 17.9%.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: On page 6, in TABLE 1. Baseline Demographics (continued), under 'No. of cycles of prior docetaxel chemotherapy' for 'Placebo Plus ADT (n = 576)', the values are 11 (1.9%) for '1-5' cycles and 91 (15.8%) for '6' cycles. Summing these gives 11 + 91 = 102 participants, and 1.9% + 15.8% = 17.7%.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The study included patients with metastatic disease either 'de novo' (synchronous) or 'after recurrence' (metachronous) as stated on page 2 under 'Patients and Treatments'. However, the results were stratified by 'disease volume (low v high) and prior docetaxel chemotherapy' (page 2) and the subgroup analysis in Figure 2B does not report results specifically by synchronous versus metachronous prognostic groups.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The study included patients with metastatic disease either 'de novo' (synchronous) or 'after recurrence' (metachronous) as stated on page 2 under 'Patients and Treatments'. However, the results were stratified by 'disease volume (low v high) and prior docetaxel chemotherapy' (page 2) and the subgroup analysis in Figure 2B does not report results specifically by synchronous versus metachronous prognostic groups.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The methods section on page 1 states that patients were 'stratified by disease volume and prior docetaxel chemotherapy'. The results section on page 1 also mentions 'Similar significant improvements in radiographic progression-free survival were reported in prespecified subgroups on the basis of disease volume'. Figure 2B, 'Subgroup' analysis, explicitly presents results for 'High volume of disease'.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The methods section on page 1 states that patients were 'stratified by disease volume and prior docetaxel chemotherapy'. The results section on page 1 also mentions 'Similar significant improvements in radiographic progression-free survival were reported in prespecified subgroups on the basis of disease volume'. Figure 2B, 'Subgroup' analysis, explicitly presents results for 'Low volume of disease'.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: Key secondary end points were time to PSA progression, time to initiation of new antineoplastic therapy (including cytotoxic and hormone therapies), PSA undetectable rate, objective response rate, time to deterioration in urinary symptoms, and OS. Other secondary end points included time to first symptomatic skeletal event, time to castration resistance, patient-reported outcomes (PROs), time to deterioration of quality of life (QoL), and time to pain progression.
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: The value was found on page 2, under the 'End Points' section, which explicitly lists 'Key secondary end points' and 'Other secondary end points'.

Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'The primary end point was radiographic progression-free survival.' This is further detailed in the 'End Points' subsection on page 2: 'The primary end point was rPFS, defined as the time from randomization to the first objective evidence of radiographic disease progression, as assessed by independent central review or death (defined as death from any cause within 24 weeks from study drug discontinuation), whichever occurred first.'

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document is a research paper reporting the results of a randomized controlled trial (ARCHES). It describes the study design, methods, results, and conclusions regarding the efficacy and safety of enzalutamide. However, it does not provide an explicit 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE)' for randomized controlled trials in general or for the evidence presented within this specific trial in the manner of a systematic review or meta-analysis that would typically include such an assessment (e.g., GRADE assessment). The document focuses on the findings of the trial itself, not an evaluation of the criteria of evidence.

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: '...men with mHSPC were randomly assigned 1:1 to enzalutamide (160 mg/day) or placebo, plus androgen deprivation therapy (ADT)...'. This indicates that enzalutamide is the additional therapy given in combination with the primary treatment, androgen deprivation therapy (ADT).

Page 4, 'Safety' section states: 'of the 14 AEs (2.4%) leading to death in the enzalutamide plus ADT group... none were assessed by the investigator to be related to treatment in the enzalutamide plus ADT group'. Grade 5 AEs are typically AEs leading to death.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | Overall.evidence: Found in Table 2, row 'Objective response rate' for 'Enzalutamide Plus ADT'. N=147, %=(147/574)*100 = 25.6%. This percentage is calculated based on the total ITT population (n=574).
